title: Jacob Jensens’s CV
text:
⚠️ NOTE: This résumé is fictional and created solely for class/project purposes. It does not represent a real employment history.
Jacob — Data Science Student Rexburg, ID Email: Legolas10004@gmail.com GitHub: https://jljensen008.github.io/jljensen008.github.io2/ LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/in/jacob-jensen-422939405
Summary Motivated data science student with hands‑on experience in Python, Polars, machine learning, and statistical analysis. Skilled in cleaning complex datasets, engineering features, building predictive models, and communicating results clearly. Strong analytical mindset with growing expertise in modern data tooling and reproducible workflows.
Technical Skills Languages: Python, SQL, Markdown Libraries & Tools: Polars, pandas, scikit‑learn, NumPy, Matplotlib, Seaborn Machine Learning: Random Forests, Logistic Regression, Train/Test Splits, Feature Engineering Data Skills: Data Cleaning, One‑Hot Encoding, Ordinal Encoding, Missing Value Imputation Other: Git/GitHub, Jupyter Notebooks, VS Code
Projects Star Wars Survey Income Prediction (Course Project) Tools: Polars, scikit‑learn, Random Forest Classifier
Cleaned and validated a 835‑row survey dataset using Polars.
Engineered ordinal variables for age, education, income, and favorability ratings.
Performed one‑hot encoding for all categorical variables.
Built a Random Forest model to predict whether household income ≥ $50,000.
Achieved 61.7% accuracy after removing data leakage and properly preparing features.
Delivered a structured client‑style report including executive summary, methods, results, and conclusions.
Data Wrangling & Feature Engineering Practice Tools: Polars, pandas
Practiced transforming messy datasets into ML‑ready formats.
Implemented mapping dictionaries, regex extraction, and categorical encoding.
Explored missing value strategies including imputation and null‑safe transformations.
Machine Learning Coursework Tools: scikit‑learn
Completed multiple assignments involving model training, evaluation, and interpretation.
Compared model performance across algorithms and preprocessing strategies.
Developed understanding of bias, variance, and model generalization.
Education Bachelor of Science — Data Science (In Progress)
Brigham Young University–Idaho Relevant coursework: Machine Learning, Data Wrangling, Statistics, Technical Writing, Biology, Chemistry
Experience (Fictional placeholder entries — replace when you have real experience)
Data Intern — Example Analytics Co. Rexburg, ID — 2025
Assisted with cleaning and preparing datasets for internal dashboards.
Automated repetitive data tasks using Python scripts.
Collaborated with analysts to validate data quality.
Student Research Assistant — Example University Lab Rexburg, ID — 2024
Supported data collection and preprocessing for a small research study.
Built simple predictive models to explore relationships in the data.
Presented findings to faculty mentors.
Certifications (Optional — add real ones later)
Python for Everybody (Coursera)
Data Science Foundations (LinkedIn Learning)
Additional Information Strong interest in machine learning, data engineering, and scientific computing.
Enjoys building clean, reproducible data pipelines.
Actively learning Polars, SQL, and cloud‑based data tools.
